name: cass
description: Coding Agent Session Search - unified CLI/TUI to index and search local coding agent history from Claude Code, Codex, Gemini, Cursor, Aider, ChatGPT, Pi-Agent, Factory, and more. Purpose-built for AI agent consumption with robot mode.
qualityReason: Vague, likely narrative or game use
capabilities: Index sessions from 11+ AI coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI, Cursor, Aider, ChatGPT, and more)
Search across all agent sessions with JSON output designed for AI consumption
Export conversations to markdown, HTML, or JSON format
Connect remote machines via SSH to search sessions across multiple computers
Timeline view to review daily or weekly agent activity
author: Dicklesworthstone
securitySummary: Legitimate developer tool for indexing and searching coding agent session history. Static findings are false positives: shell backticks in documentation examples, AES-256-GCM encryption documentation, and standard dev path access for indexing. No malicious intent detected.
valueStatement: Developers lose track of solutions found by AI coding assistants. This tool indexes sessions from Claude Code, Codex, Gemini, Cursor, and 8+ other agents into a unified searchable database.
limitations: Requires local agent session data to be present on disk
ChatGPT v2/v3 encryption requires keychain access for indexing
Semantic search requires manual model installation
TUI mode blocks terminal; must use --robot flag for automation

useCases.targetUser: Developers working with AI coding assistants
useCases.title: Find past solutions
useCases.description: Search your entire agent history to find solutions you discovered before. Query by error message, function name, or concept.

useCases.title: Cross-agent research
useCases.description: Compare how different AI agents approached the same problem. See patterns in their solutions and learn from their different strategies.

useCases.targetUser: Development teams using multiple AI tools
useCases.title: Team handoff
useCases.description: Share relevant past sessions with team members. Export context so the next agent understands what has already been tried.

promptTemplates.title: Basic search
promptTemplates.prompt: Search my agent sessions for solutions related to #{query}. Use cass search "#{query}" --robot --limit 10 --days 30. Report any relevant code patterns or commands that worked.
promptTemplates.scenario: When you need to find how you solved a similar problem before

promptTemplates.title: Error resolution
promptTemplates.prompt: Find all sessions where I dealt with #{error_type}. Use cass search "#{error_type}" --robot --fields summary. Show me the approaches that succeeded.
promptTemplates.scenario: When encountering a new error and wanting to see past solutions

promptTemplates.title: Timeline review
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me my agent activity for today. Use cass timeline --today --json --group-by hour. Summarize what I worked on.
promptTemplates.scenario: When reviewing daily progress or documenting work

promptTemplates.title: Agent comparison
promptTemplates.prompt: Compare how Claude Code and Cursor handled #{topic}. Search both agents separately and show me their different approaches.
promptTemplates.scenario: When comparing strategies across different AI coding tools

bestPractices: Run cass index --full after installing to ensure complete session coverage
Use --robot flag for all AI-to-AI interactions to get structured JSON output
Enable watch mode (cass index --watch) for real-time indexing during development
Configure remote sources to search sessions from all your development machines
antiPatterns: Running cass without --robot flag in automated workflows (blocks terminal)
Skipping regular indexing after agent session data changes
Using imprecise search terms that return too many unrelated results
Ignoring the --limit flag when working with limited token budgets

faq.question: Which agents does CASS support?
faq.answer: CASS supports Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI, Cline, OpenCode, Amp, Cursor, ChatGPT, Aider, Pi-Agent, and Factory (Droid). Each agent's sessions are stored in their native format and location.

faq.question: How does robot mode differ from TUI mode?
faq.answer: Robot mode (--robot flag) outputs structured JSON designed for AI consumption. TUI mode launches an interactive terminal interface for human users. Always use --robot in automated workflows.

faq.question: Can CASS search remote machines?
faq.answer: Yes, CASS supports remote sources via SSH. Run cass sources setup to discover hosts from ~/.ssh/config, then sync sessions from remote machines to search locally.

faq.question: What happens to encrypted ChatGPT sessions?
faq.answer: ChatGPT v2/v3 uses AES-256-GCM encryption with keys stored in the macOS keychain. CASS can only index these if keychain access is granted. Legacy v1 sessions are unencrypted and indexed automatically.

faq.question: Does CASS send data to the cloud?
faq.answer: No, CASS is a local-only tool. All indexing and searching happens on your machine. Semantic search models must be installed manually and never downloaded automatically.

faq.question: How much disk space does indexing require?
faq.answer: CASS uses SQLite for the index (typically 10-50MB for extensive histories) and stores minimal metadata. Your original agent session files remain unchanged.
